narrative.headline: 2020 Volkswagen Atlas: electrical gripes lead the complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 25 complaints with NHTSA. The electrical system is the most reported area at 44%, followed by the air bags (32%) and forward collision avoidance (28%). Steering (20%) and service brakes (16%) round out the list. None of these complaints report a crash, fire, injury, or death.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The top complaint: 11 reports (44%). No crashes, fires, or injuries reported, but it is the area owners flag most often.

issues.sample: MY 2020 VOLKSWAGEN ATLAS HAS BEEN IN AND OUT OF THE SHOP.  WE PURCHASED THIS VEHICLE IN APRIL 2020 WITH GREAT HOPES...WE'VE BEEN LOYAL VW CUSTOMERS AND HAVE OWNED 2 OTHER VW VEHICLES PRIOR.  ISSUES BEGAN MID-AUG 2020, THE CHECK ENGINE LIGHT TURNED ON AND WE BEGAN HEARING A RATTLI
issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 8 reports (32%). None involved a crash or injury, but air bags are a safety system worth confirming work properly.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: Third most reported: 7 reports (28%). No crashes or injuries reported. Check that the collision warning and braking assist behave as expected.

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: 5 reports (20%), with no crashes or injuries reported. Worth a feel on a test drive for anything unusual in the steering.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: The fewest complaints here: 4 reports (16%), with no crashes or injuries reported. Still good to test the brakes during a drive.
